item.title: Ānanda: Going forth, I praise it as the Dhamma-eye. After examining, the Lord approved the going forth. Bondage is the household life, gathering dust; Freedom…
item.description:
Ānanda:
Going forth, I praise it as the Dhamma-eye.
After examining, the Lord approved the going forth.

Bondage is the household life, gathering dust;
Freedom is the homeless life; seeing this, he went forth.

After going forth, he avoided evil bodily deeds,
Abandoned evil speech, and purified his livelihood.

From: Sutta Nipāta 3.1
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Elder Kosalavihāri: With faith, I went forth, living homeless. In the forest, a hut was built for me. I live heedfully, ardent, mindful, clearly comprehending.
item.description:
Elder Kosalavihāri:

With faith, I went forth, living homeless.
In the forest, a hut was built for me.
I live heedfully, ardent, mindful, clearly comprehending.

From: Theragāthā 1.59
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Here, bhikkhus, in whatever assembly, when the discourses spoken by the Tathāgata, deep, profound, transcendent, connected with the void, are being taught, the bhikkhus do…
item.description:
Here, bhikkhus, in whatever assembly, when the discourses spoken by the Tathāgata, deep, profound, transcendent, connected with the void, are being taught, the bhikkhus do not listen well, do not lend an ear, do not apply their minds to understand them, and they do not think that these teachings should be learned and memorized. But when the poetic discourses composed by poets, with beautiful vowels and consonants, of outsiders, spoken by disciples of outsiders, are being taught, they listen well, lend an ear, apply their minds to understand them, and they think that these teachings should be learned and memorized. This, bhikkhus, is called an assembly trained in display, not trained in questioning.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 2.47
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, there are these four perversions of perception, perversions of mind, perversions of view. What four? Regarding the impermanent as permanent, bhikkhus, that is a…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, there are these four perversions of perception, perversions of mind, perversions of view. What four? Regarding the impermanent as permanent, bhikkhus, that is a perversion of perception, perversion of mind, perversion of view. Regarding the painful as pleasurable, bhikkhus, that is a perversion of perception, perversion of mind, perversion of view. Regarding the non-self as self, bhikkhus, that is a perversion of perception, perversion of mind, perversion of view. Regarding the impure as pure, bhikkhus, that is a perversion of perception, perversion of mind, perversion of view.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 4.49
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: For one without craving, a bhikkhu who has cut the stream, Who has abandoned good and evil deeds, there is no sorrow. The Blessed One…
item.description:
For one without craving, a bhikkhu who has cut the stream,
Who has abandoned good and evil deeds, there is no sorrow.

The Blessed One further said:
I teach you the knowledge of the sage's practice.
Practice like a razor, pressing the tongue against the palate,
Restrain your belly.

Let your mind not be sluggish, but do not think too much.
Without defilement, independent, living the supreme holy life.

Train to sit alone, living the right ascetic life.
Living alone is called the sage's practice.
If you find comfort in solitude, that is good.

Having heard the voices of the wise, shine in the ten directions,
Practice jhāna, abandon sensual desires,
That my disciples may grow in mind and faith.

From: Sutta Nipāta 3.11
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Here, friends, a person has no defilements but does not truly understand: 'There are no defilements within me.' For such a person, this can be…
item.description:
Here, friends, a person has no defilements but does not truly understand: 'There are no defilements within me.' For such a person, this can be expected: 'He will give attention to the sign of the beautiful. By giving attention to the sign of the beautiful, greed will defile his mind. He will die while still having greed, still having hatred, still having delusion, still having defilements, still having a defiled mind.' Friends, just as a bronze bowl, brought from the market or from the smithy, pure and clean, and its owner does not use it, does not clean it, and throws it into a dusty place. Friends, after a time, that bowl becomes even more defiled, even dustier.

From: Majjhima Nikāya 5 – Anaṅgaṇasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Bhikkhus, there are these three internal stains, internal enemies, internal opponents, internal murderers, internal adversaries. What three? Greed, bhikkhus, is an internal stain, internal enemy,…
item.description:
Bhikkhus, there are these three internal stains, internal enemies, internal opponents, internal murderers, internal adversaries. What three? Greed, bhikkhus, is an internal stain, internal enemy, internal opponent, internal murderer, internal adversary. Hatred, bhikkhus, is an internal stain, internal enemy, internal opponent, internal murderer, internal adversary. Delusion, bhikkhus, is an internal stain, internal enemy, internal opponent, internal murderer, internal adversary. These are the three internal stains, internal enemies, internal opponents, internal murderers, internal adversaries.

From: Itivuttaka 88
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: Young householder, in five ways a gentleman should attend on samaṇas and brahmins as the upper direction: acting with loving-kindness in body, acting with loving-kindness…
item.description:
Young householder, in five ways a gentleman should attend on samaṇas and brahmins as the upper direction: acting with loving-kindness in body, acting with loving-kindness in speech, acting with loving-kindness in mind, keeping open house for them, and supplying their material needs. Young householder, when the gentleman attends on samaṇas and brahmins as the upper direction in these five ways, the samaṇas and brahmins have compassion for him in five ways: they restrain him from evil, they encourage him to do good, they have compassion for him with a mind of loving-kindness, they teach him what he has not heard, they purify what he has heard, and they point out the path to heaven.

From: Dīgha Nikāya 31 – Siṅgālasutta
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu complete in beauty? Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu observes the precepts, lives restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, perfect…
item.description:
And, bhikkhus, how is a bhikkhu complete in beauty?
Here, bhikkhus, a bhikkhu observes the precepts, lives restrained by the restraint of the Pātimokkha, perfect in conduct and resort, seeing danger in the slightest faults, he undertakes and trains in the training rules. Thus, bhikkhus, is a bhikkhu complete in beauty.

From: Aṅguttara Nikāya 3.96
📖 Read this sutta

item.title: My mind has traveled all around, To every direction, Yet I have not found anything Dearer than myself. Since the self is so dear to…
item.description:
My mind has traveled all around,
To every direction,
Yet I have not found anything
Dearer than myself.

Since the self is so dear to everyone,
Therefore, one who loves oneself
Should not harm another's self.

From: Udāna 5.1
📖 Read this sutta
